cards.title: Gut Microbiome — Overview & Key Phyla

alerts.label: Core concept
alerts.text: The gut microbiome contains ~38 trillion microorganisms — equal to the number of human cells. 99% of microbial genes are bacterial. Diversity is the single strongest indicator of microbiome health.

pearls.label: 🟡 F:B Ratio
pearls.text: Firmicutes:Bacteroidetes ratio. Elevated in obesity and type 2 diabetes. Dietary fibre ↑ Bacteroidetes. High-fat/sugar diet ↑ Firmicutes. F:B ratio can shift within days of dietary change — the microbiome is remarkably plastic.

cards._legacyHtml:

🟡 F:B Ratio
Firmicutes:Bacteroidetes ratio. Elevated in obesity and type 2 diabetes. Dietary fibre ↑ Bacteroidetes. High-fat/sugar diet ↑ Firmicutes. F:B ratio can shift within days of dietary change — the microbiome is remarkably plastic.

cards.title: Leaky Gut (Intestinal Permeability)

alerts.label: Definition
alerts.text: Increased intestinal permeability = breakdown of tight junction proteins (occludin, claudin, ZO-1) allowing antigens, LPS, and microbes into systemic circulation → chronic low-grade inflammation.

pearls.label: 🟡 Clinical Pearl
pearls.text: L-Glutamine 5g twice daily is the best-evidenced supplement for tight junction repair. Zinc carnosine and butyrate (sodium butyrate 600mg/day) are strong adjuncts. Avoid NSAIDs throughout the repair phase — a single dose of ibuprofen can unmeasurably increase permeability within 1 hour.

steps.title: Triggers
steps.text: — NSAIDs, alcohol, gluten (in sensitive individuals), stress (cortisol), dysbiosis, food antigens, zonulin dysregulation

steps.title: Consequences
steps.text: — Systemic LPS (endotoxaemia) → TLR4 activation → TNF-α, IL-6 → insulin resistance, neuroinflammation, autoimmunity

steps.title: Assessment
steps.text: — Lactulose:mannitol ratio (urine), serum zonulin, LPS-binding protein, faecal calprotectin (inflammation marker)

steps.title: Repair protocol (5R)
steps.text: — Remove triggers → Replace (enzymes, HCl) → Re-inoculate (probiotics) → Repair (glutamine, zinc, butyrate) → Rebalance (stress, sleep)

cards.title: SIBO — Small Intestinal Bacterial Overgrowth

pearls.label: 🟡 Naturopathic approach
pearls.text: Herbal antimicrobials equal to rifaximin in studies: berberine, oregano oil, allicin (garlic extract). Prokinetics essential post-treatment (ginger, 5-HTP, low-dose naltrexone) to prevent recurrence — MMC (migrating motor complex) dysfunction is the root cause in most cases. Elemental diet for 2 weeks achieves 80% eradication.

cards.title: Dysbiosis Patterns & Clinical Correlations

pearls.label: 🟡 Faecalibacterium prausnitzii
pearls.text: The most important anti-inflammatory bacterium in the gut. Produces butyrate directly. Inversely correlated with IBD severity, depression scores, and metabolic syndrome. Restored by: dietary fibre (especially inulin, FOS), polyphenols (berries, green tea), and avoiding emulsifiers (polysorbate-80, carboxymethylcellulose).

cards.title: Probiotics — Evidence-Based Guide

alerts.label: Key rule
alerts.text: Probiotics are strain-specific — not interchangeable. The evidence for "Lactobacillus" does NOT apply across all Lactobacillus species or strains. Always cite the specific strain when recommending.

rows: AAD ★, traveller's diarrhoea, eczema in infants
C. diff prevention ★, AAD, IBD adjunct

pearls.label: 🟡 Timing matters
pearls.text: Take probiotics with or just before a meal (fat/protein slows gastric emptying) — survival to colon increases 4-fold compared to fasting. Refrigerated strains must stay cold. If using with antibiotics: take 2 hours apart, continue for 4 weeks post-course. L. rhamnosus GG + S. boulardii together is the most evidence-based combo for AAD prevention.

cards.title: Prebiotics, Postbiotics & Synbiotics

rows: Non-digestible fibre selectively feeding beneficial microbes
Inulin (chicory), FOS (onion, garlic), GOS (legumes), resistant starch (cooled rice, green banana)

pearls.label: 🟡 Butyrate — the master metabolite
pearls.text: Butyrate is the preferred fuel of colonocytes, suppresses NF-κB, repairs tight junctions, induces regulatory T-cells, and inhibits histone deacetylase (epigenetic anti-cancer effect). Produced from resistant starch by Roseburia, F. prausnitzii, and Eubacterium rectale. Target: 50g fibre/day from diverse plant sources = best butyrate strategy.

steps.title: Lifestyle First
steps.text: Elevate head of bed 15–20cm (most effective for night symptoms). Left lateral sleeping. No eating within 3 hours of bed. Small frequent meals. Weight loss (each BMI unit reduction ↓ GERD risk 10%). Stop smoking.

steps.title: Triggers to Eliminate
steps.text: Chocolate, coffee, alcohol, citrus, tomatoes, fatty/fried foods (delay gastric emptying). Peppermint relaxes LOS — avoid in GERD.

steps.title: Naturopathic Treatments
steps.text: DGL liquorice 380mg before meals (stimulates mucus, protects oesophagus). Aloe vera juice 50ml AC. Slippery elm 400mg before meals (demulcent). Alkaline water pH 8.8 (denatures pepsin). Digestive enzymes if hypochlorhydria suspected.

cards.title: Coeliac Disease, Haemorrhoids & Liver Health

alerts.label: CRITICAL ★ — Do NOT start GFD before testing
alerts.text: GFD normalises antibodies and biopsy — making diagnosis impossible. Test FIRST. If already on GFD: 6g gluten/day × 6 weeks challenge required.

rows: First-line. Always check total IgA alongside — IgA deficiency gives false negative (2% of coeliac)
Must eat gluten before. Min 4 biopsies. Marsh grading 0→3 (villous atrophy = diagnostic)
If ABSENT = coeliac very unlikely. Rules out coeliac in equivocal cases.

pearls.label: 🟡 Nutritional Replenishment After Diagnosis
pearls.text: Commonly deficient at diagnosis: iron (villous atrophy → malabsorption), folate, B12, Vit D, calcium, zinc, magnesium. Check ALL at diagnosis and replenish. GFD allows mucosa to heal and absorption to recover within 3–6 months. Annual monitoring bloods thereafter. Avoid: cross-contamination (shared cooking surfaces, toasters, condiment jars). Read all labels — gluten hides in sauces, soups, medications, soya sauce, malt extract.

pearls.label: 🟡 Hepatoprotective Foods
pearls.text: Coffee ★★★★: 2–3 cups/day reduces liver fibrosis 40%, cirrhosis risk 30%, HCC risk 40%. Mechanism: kahweol + cafestol are antifibrotic; caffeine reduces hepatic stellate cell activation. Green tea EGCG: 3–5 cups/day antioxidant + hepatoprotective. Broccoli sprouts: sulforaphane — Phase 2 enzyme induction. Garlic: allicin — antioxidant, hepatoprotective. Walnuts: omega-3, glutathione precursors, arginine (supports ammonia detoxification). Avoid completely: alcohol, high-fructose foods, processed foods high in trans fats.

steps.title: Protocol
steps.text: Psyllium husk 5g BD ★ (most effective single intervention). Hydration 3L/day. Sitz bath warm 38°C 15 min TDS. Witch hazel topical (astringent, reduces bleeding). Horse chestnut extract (aescin) 300mg BD (improves venous tone). Squat position for defecation ★ (reduces straining dramatically). Butcher's broom 150mg TDS. Refer if Grade 3–4 or rectal bleeding in anyone over 40 (colonoscopy to exclude cancer).

cards.title: GERD — Naturopathic Assessment & Protocol

alerts.label: Alarm Features — Refer for Endoscopy Urgently
alerts.text: Dysphagia (difficulty swallowing), odynophagia (painful swallowing), unexplained weight loss, age over 55 with new onset dyspepsia, haematemesis or melaena. These require endoscopy before any naturopathic treatment.

pearls.label: 🟡 PPI Deprescribing
pearls.text: 70% of long-term PPI prescriptions have no ongoing indication. Step down: OD to every other day x 4 weeks, then H2 blocker x 4 weeks, then PRN antacid. Rebound acid hypersecretion occurs 2-4 weeks after stopping — warn patients this is temporary, not their original disease returning. DGL 380mg before meals throughout taper prevents symptom relapse significantly.

steps.title: Lifestyle First Line
steps.text: Elevate head of bed 15-20cm — most effective for nocturnal symptoms. Left lateral sleeping position. No eating within 3 hours of bed. Small frequent meals. Weight loss (each BMI unit reduction lowers GERD risk 10%). Stop smoking — dramatically reduces lower oesophageal sphincter pressure.

steps.title: Dietary Triggers
steps.text: Eliminate: chocolate, coffee and tea, alcohol, citrus fruits, tomatoes, fatty and fried foods. Peppermint relaxes LOS — avoid in GERD. Spicy food variable — not proven to worsen in RCTs but subjectively triggers in many patients.

steps.title: Naturopathic Treatments
steps.text: DGL liquorice 380mg before meals (stimulates mucus production, protects oesophageal mucosa). Aloe vera juice 50ml before meals (anti-inflammatory — small RCT positive). Slippery elm 400mg with water before meals (demulcent, coats oesophagus). Alkaline water pH 8.8 (denatures pepsin — pepsin is the main oesophageal irritant).

cards.title: Coeliac Disease — Diagnosis & Nutritional Management

alerts.label: Do NOT Start GFD Before Testing
alerts.text: A gluten-free diet normalises the diagnostic antibodies and biopsy findings — making diagnosis impossible. Always complete testing first. If patient has already started GFD, a gluten challenge (6g/day for 6 weeks) is required before testing.

rows: First-line test. ALWAYS check total IgA alongside — IgA deficiency gives false negative (occurs in 2% of coeliac).
If IgA deficient: use TTG-IgG or deamidated gliadin peptide IgG instead
Must still be eating gluten before biopsy. Marsh grading 0-3. Minimum 4 biopsies (patchy distribution).
Present in 99% of coeliac — if ABSENT, coeliac very unlikely. Used to rule OUT in equivocal cases.

steps.title: Gluten-Free Diet — What to Avoid
steps.text: ALL wheat (bread, pasta, cakes, chapati, roti, naan, soya sauce, beer), barley (barley water, malt extract), rye. SAFE: rice, corn, potato, quinoa, millet, sorghum, certified GF oats. Read ALL labels — gluten hides in soups, sauces, processed meats, medications, shared fryer chips.

steps.title: Nutritional Replenishment
steps.text: Commonly deficient at diagnosis: iron, folate, B12, Vit D3, calcium, zinc, magnesium. Check all at diagnosis. Iron bisglycinate 25mg with Vit C. Vit D3 4000 IU. Calcium 1200mg. B12 sublingual if low. Annual monitoring bloods thereafter.

cards.title: Candida & Fungal Overgrowth — Assessment & Protocol

alerts.label: Candida Overgrowth — Overdiagnosed but Real
alerts.text: True Candida overgrowth (dysbiosis) exists — primarily in immunocompromised, post-antibiotic, or high-sugar-diet patients. Many "Candida" diagnoses are not supported by evidence. The clinical approach: diagnose carefully (stool culture, organic acids), treat with evidence-based antifungals, and address root causes.

rows: Recurrent vaginal thrush, oral thrush, nail fungal
Bloating, gas, fatigue, brain fog, sugar cravings
Comprehensive stool analysis with fungal culture

steps.title: Antifungal Protocol
steps.text: Caprylic acid 1–2g TID ★★ (coconut-derived C8 — disrupts fungal cell membrane). Undecylenic acid 500mg TID ★★. Oregano oil (carvacrol 75%) 200mg TID ★★★ — potent antifungal, anti-biofilm. Grapefruit seed extract 250mg TID ★★. Berberine 500mg TID ★★ (anti-Candida + glucose regulation — removes substrate). Saccharomyces boulardii 5×10⁹ CFU ★★★ — competitive exclusion. Rotation every 2–3 weeks prevents resistance.

steps.title: Anti-Candida Diet
steps.text: Remove: all sugars (including fruit juice, honey, alcohol), refined carbohydrates, yeast-containing foods (bread, beer, fermented foods — for first 4 weeks). Add: antifungal foods: garlic ★★★ (allicin), coconut oil (caprylic acid), raw apple cider vinegar (malic acid), cruciferous vegetables, olive oil. Duration: strict phase 4 weeks, then gradual reintroduction. Die-off reaction (Herxheimer): fatigue, headache worsening in first week — is expected, manage with hydration, activated charcoal 30 min away from supplements.

cards.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease — Naturopathic Adjunct

alerts.label: IBD — Naturopathic as Adjunct to Conventional Only
alerts.text: Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis are serious immune-mediated conditions requiring gastroenterologist co-management. Biologics and immunomodulators prevent complications and surgery. Naturopathic role: reduce flare frequency, improve nutritional status, manage symptoms, support quality of life, and address lifestyle factors.

rows: ★★★ First-line induction for paediatric Crohn's — equivalent to corticosteroids
Elemental or polymeric formula as sole nutrition 6–8 weeks. BNYS: support adherence, introduce foods carefully after.
★★ IBD-AID trial: significant improvement in Crohn's and UC symptom scores
Eliminates: grains, refined sugars, most dairy. Includes: well-cooked vegetables, homemade yogurt, specific fruits. Strict 3–12 months.
★★★ UC maintenance: VSL#3 equivalent to mesalazine for mild-moderate UC remission maintenance
VSL#3 1 sachet BD. Contains 450 billion bacteria from 8 strains. Refrigerated. Continue through remission.
★★★ UC: curcumin 2g/day added to mesalazine significantly improves remission rates vs mesalazine alone
Curcumin 1g BD with piperine. BCM-95 formulation preferred (enhanced bioavailability). With meals.
★★ Crohn's maintenance: omega-3 reduces relapse rate in some RCTs
EPA+DHA 3–4g/day. Enteric-coated capsules reduce fishy burps and GI side effects.

pearls.label: 🟡 Nutritional Deficiency Screening in IBD
pearls.text: IBD causes malabsorption of almost everything. Screen and supplement: Iron (check ferritin — IV iron preferred over oral in active IBD — oral worsens mucosal inflammation), B12 methylcobalamin (ileal Crohn's — intrinsic factor absorption site), Vit D (deficiency worsens IBD prognosis and flare frequency — target 100–150 nmol/L), Zinc (depleted in diarrhoea — 25mg/day), Folate (methotrexate-induced depletion), Magnesium. Monitor: FBC, B12, folate, iron studies, Vit D, zinc, albumin every 6 months.

cards.title: Constipation — Comprehensive Naturopathic Protocol

alerts.label: Constipation — The Rome IV Definition
alerts.text: Rome IV: ≥2 of the following for ≥3 months (onset 6+ months ago): straining >25% of defecations, lumpy/hard stools, sensation of incomplete evacuation, sensation of anorectal obstruction, manual manoeuvres to facilitate >25% of defecations, <3 spontaneous defecations per week. Naturopathic management addresses all 6 subtypes.

rows: Normal colonic transit, low fibre, dehydration, inactivity
Reduced colonic propulsive motility (often: hypothyroid, opioids, DM neuropathy)
Treat underlying cause. Senna stimulant laxative. Probiotics (Bifidobacterium lactis). Triphala ★★.
Paradoxical external sphincter contraction on straining
Biofeedback therapy ★★★ (90% success rate). Pelvic floor physiotherapy. Squatting position.
Low-FODMAP ★★★, peppermint oil ★★★, gut-directed hypnotherapy ★★★, magnesium oxide

steps.title: First-Line Protocol
steps.text: Fibre: psyllium husk 5–10g BD with water ★★★ (best evidence — also reduces cholesterol). Flaxseed 1 tbsp/day (ground — add to food). Prunes 3–6/day ★★★ (sorbitol + fibre + chlorogenic acid — RCT equivalent to psyllium). Hydration: 2L water/day minimum — dehydration is the #1 overlooked cause. Position: Squatty Potty (7–9 inch stool) ★★★ — puborectalis muscle relaxes in squat, reducing anorectal angle. Walking 30 min/day ★★★ (colonic propulsion increases with exercise).

steps.title: Herbal & Supplement Protocol
steps.text: Triphala 500mg BD ★★★ (trifruit Ayurvedic laxative — safe long-term, improves gut microbiome). Magnesium oxide 300–500mg at night ★★★ (osmotic — draws water into colon). Senna 15mg at night (stimulant — for short-term use; Triphala for long-term). Castor oil pack abdomen (traditional — stimulates peristalsis via ricinoleic acid). Probiotics: Bifidobacterium lactis HN019 ★★★ (reduces colonic transit time). Cascara sagrada (anthraquinone — use cautiously, max 1 week).

cards.title: Gut-Brain Axis — Mechanisms

alerts.label: Definition
alerts.text: The gut-brain axis is a bidirectional communication network linking the enteric nervous system (ENS), vagus nerve, HPA axis, immune system, and gut microbiome to the central nervous system.

rows: Gut microbiome signals reach brainstem directly
Stress → leaky gut → more stress (vicious cycle)

pearls.label: 🟡 Psychobiotics
pearls.text: Psychobiotics = probiotics with demonstrated mental health benefits. Best evidence: L. rhamnosus JB-1 (anxiety, GABA signalling via vagus), L. helveticus R0052 + B. longum R0175 (stress, cortisol, Lallemand formulation studied in RCTs), Lactobacillus casei Shirota (mood, sleep). Mechanism: vagus nerve, tryptophan/serotonin axis, GABA production.

cards.title: Stool Analysis — Comprehensive Interpretation

pearls.label: 🟡 Elevated beta-glucuronidase
pearls.text: Beta-glucuronidase re-activates conjugated oestrogens in the colon → increased oestrogen reabsorption → oestrogen dominance → risk of breast, endometrial, and prostate cancers. Calcium D-glucarate (500mg twice daily) inhibits beta-glucuronidase and is a key supplement for oestrogen-driven conditions. Also lowered by Lactobacillus acidophilus and a high-fibre diet.

cards.title: SIBO — Diagnosis, Types & Naturopathic Treatment

rows: Bloating worst after carbs ★, belching, flatulence, altered bowel habit, brain fog, fatigue, B12/iron/fat-soluble vitamin deficiency

Lactulose or glucose substrate. H2 rise >20ppm above baseline within 90 min = positive. 3-hour test required to detect methane. Prepare: no antibiotics for 4 weeks, no probiotics for 1 week, low-FODMAP diet for 24h.
Rifaximin 550mg TDS × 14d (H2). Add neomycin for methane. 70–80% eradication rate.
Allicin (garlic extract) 450mg TDS — comparable to rifaximin (RCT). Oregano oil, berberine, neem. Elemental diet × 14 days (80–85% eradication — best evidence). Biofilm disruptors: NAC, serrapeptase.
Prokinetics: ginger 200mg before bed, 5-HTP 50mg at night, 4–5h between meals (no snacking), treat root cause (hypothyroidism, PPI, adhesions, MMC dysfunction)

pearls.label: 🟡 The MMC — why SIBO recurs without prokinetics
pearls.text: The migrating motor complex sweeps bacteria from the small intestine every 90–120 min ONLY during fasting. Frequent snacking, low thyroid, opioids, stress and PPI use impair MMC → bacterial accumulation → relapse. Prokinetics are non-negotiable for long-term SIBO management.

cards.title: Liver Disease — NAFLD to Cirrhosis

alerts.label: NAFLD — The Silent Epidemic
alerts.text: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) affects 25% of the global population and 30–40% of Indian adults. The spectrum: simple steatosis → NASH (with inflammation + fibrosis) → cirrhosis → hepatocellular carcinoma. NAFLD is strongly linked to insulin resistance — treat the root cause, not just the liver. BNYS is excellently positioned for NAFLD management.

rows: Often asymptomatic. ALT mildly ↑. Bright liver on ultrasound.
Weight loss 7–10% ★★★ (resolves steatosis). Low-fructose diet. Exercise. Berberine.
Fatigue, RUQ discomfort. ALT/AST ↑↑. FIB-4 score elevated.
Above + silymarin ★★★, Vit E 800IU/day ★★★ (PIVENS trial), omega-3, TUDCA, curcumin.
Hepatologist co-management. Naturopathic supportive: protein 1.2g/kg, zinc, magnesium, BCAA, low-sodium diet. Avoid hepatotoxic herbs.

pearls.label: 🟡 Coffee — Nature's Hepatoprotective Drink
pearls.text: 3–4 cups of coffee per day reduces: NAFLD risk 40%, cirrhosis risk 40%, liver cancer risk 40%, liver fibrosis progression 50%. The protective effects are from: chlorogenic acids (antioxidant), cafestol (NRF2 activation), caffeine (reversal of hepatic fibrosis in animal models). Both caffeinated and decaffeinated coffee are protective — but caffeinated shows stronger effects. Green tea catechins: also hepatoprotective (EGCG 400mg/day reduces liver enzymes in NAFLD ★★). This is the most evidence-based dietary recommendation in hepatology.

steps.title: NAFLD Naturopathic Protocol
steps.text: Silymarin (milk thistle) standardised to 70–80% silymarin 140mg TID ★★★ (reduces ALT/AST, improves histology in multiple RCTs). Berberine 500mg TID ★★★ (reduces hepatic fat via AMPK activation — comparable to pioglitazone in NASH RCT). Vit E (natural d-alpha tocopherol) 800 IU/day ★★★ (PIVENS trial: significant NASH histological improvement). TUDCA (tauroursodeoxycholic acid) 500mg BD ★★ (bile acid that reduces ER stress in hepatocytes). Omega-3 EPA+DHA 3–4g/day (reduces hepatic triglyceride by 15–20%). Curcumin 1000mg with piperine BD (anti-inflammatory, antifibrotic). Coffee 3+ cups/day ★★★ (most replicated hepatoprotective food — reduces fibrosis progression 40–50%).

steps.title: FIB-4 Score — Non-Invasive Fibrosis Assessment
steps.text: FIB-4 = (Age × AST) ÷ (Platelets × √ALT). <1.3: low fibrosis risk (F0–F1) — continue naturopathic management. 1.3–2.67: intermediate — FibroScan (transient elastography) recommended. >2.67: high risk of advanced fibrosis — hepatologist referral. Use FIB-4 at baseline and every 12 months to track treatment response. Free calculator available online. Avoids biopsy in most patients.

cards.title: Functional Dyspepsia & Gastroparesis

alerts.label: Functional Dyspepsia — Rome IV Criteria
alerts.text: Functional dyspepsia (FD) affects 20% of adults globally. Rome IV criteria: Bothersome postprandial fullness, early satiation, epigastric pain/burning — for 3+ months, no structural cause on endoscopy. Two subtypes: (1) Postprandial Distress Syndrome (PDS) — meal-related symptoms. (2) Epigastric Pain Syndrome (EPS) — pain/burning not meal-related. BNYS evidence base is strong for FD.

rows: ★★★ Multiple RCTs — comparable to cisapride for FD/IBS

Dual action: prokinetic (speeds gastric emptying) + spasmolytic (relaxes smooth muscle). Multi-herb synergy superior to individual herbs.

pearls.label: 🟡 Gastroparesis — Delayed Gastric Emptying
pearls.text: Gastroparesis: delayed gastric emptying without mechanical obstruction. Symptoms: postprandial fullness, nausea, vomiting undigested food, bloating, early satiety. Causes: diabetes (autonomic neuropathy) ★, post-viral, post-surgical, idiopathic. Diagnosis: gastric emptying scintigraphy (gold standard) or 13C-spirulina breath test. Naturopathic: Ginger ★★★ (improves antral contractions), Iberogast ★★, small frequent meals (6/day), low-fat/low-fibre diet (reduces gastric emptying delay), liquid/pureed meals, no carbonated drinks, upright posture after meals. Refer gastroenterology if: significant weight loss, recurrent vomiting, dehydration.

cards.title: Pancreatitis — Acute & Chronic

alerts.label: Acute Pancreatitis — Medical Emergency
alerts.text: Acute pancreatitis requires immediate hospital management. BNYS practitioners must recognise it and refer urgently. Never manage acute pancreatitis naturopathically — even mild cases require IV fluids, nil by mouth, and monitoring for complications (necrosis, ARDS, multi-organ failure).

rows: Mild: no organ failure, no local complications. Moderately severe: transient organ failure (<48hrs) or local complications. Severe: persistent organ failure (>48hrs)
PaO₂ <60mmHg, Age >55, Neutrophils >15,000, Calcium <2mmol/L, renal: urea >16mmol/L, Enzymes (LDH >600, AST >200), Albumin <32g/L, Sugar (glucose >10mmol/L)
PANCREAS mnemonic. ≥3 within 48hrs = severe. Predicts ICU need.
I: Idiopathic. G: Gallstones ★ (40%). E: Ethanol ★ (30%). T: Trauma, Triglycerides (>11mmol/L), drugs, hypercalcaemia, ERCP, viral
Gallstones and alcohol = 70% of cases. TG >11 = severe hypertriglyceridaemic pancreatitis (treat TG with fibrates + omega-3)

steps.title: Chronic Pancreatitis — Naturopathic Support
steps.text: Enzyme replacement: pancreatic enzymes (lipase 25,000–50,000 IU) with every meal and snack — essential for fat digestion and malabsorption. Fat-soluble vitamin supplementation: A, D, E, K2 — all malabsorbed in exocrine insufficiency. Medium-chain triglycerides (MCT oil): absorbed without pancreatic enzymes — use instead of LCT fats. Antioxidant protocol: Vit C 1g, Vit E 400IU, selenium 200mcg, methionine 2g, beta-carotene 9000IU ★★ — reduces pain episodes (Banerjee antioxidant cocktail). Complete alcohol cessation ★★★ — most important modifiable factor for progression. Smoking cessation ★★★ (doubles progression rate to carcinoma). Pain management: curcumin, acupuncture.

cards.title: Colorectal Cancer Prevention

alerts.label: CRC — 90% Preventable With Lifestyle + Screening
alerts.text: Colorectal cancer is the 3rd most common cancer globally. 90% arise from adenomatous polyps over 10–15 years — a long preventable window. BNYS practitioners can powerfully reduce patient CRC risk through dietary and lifestyle prescription, and ensure appropriate screening referrals.

rows: 150 min moderate exercise/week ★★★ (reduces CRC risk 24%). Exercise also improves survival post-diagnosis.
Reduce to <1 drink/day or abstain. Folate partially mitigates alcohol-related CRC risk.
Limit processed meat (biggest risk — hot dogs, sausage, bacon). Replace red meat with fish, poultry, legumes.
Dietary fibre 30–35g/day ★★★. Every 10g extra fibre = 10% ↓ CRC risk. Whole grains, legumes, vegetables.
Calcium 1000mg/day from food + supplement ★★ (reduces polyp recurrence). Vit D 1000–4000 IU (improves calcium effect).

steps.title: The Gut Microbiome & CRC Connection
steps.text: Fusobacterium nucleatum found in 90% of CRC tumours — promotes tumour growth via FadA adhesin + Wnt signalling. High fibre diet selects against Fusobacterium and enriches butyrate-producing bacteria (Faecalibacterium prausnitzii, Roseburia). Butyrate is the primary colonocyte fuel AND a histone deacetylase inhibitor — epigenetically suppresses tumour genes. Resistant starch (cooked-cooled rice and potatoes, green bananas) is the most potent butyrate substrate — fermented selectively by protective bacteria. Target: diverse, high-plant, high-fibre microbiome.

steps.title: CRC Screening — Ensure Patients Are Screened
steps.text: Indian recommendations (ICMR): colonoscopy every 10 years from age 45 (40 if family history). Faecal immunochemical test (FIT): annually — non-invasive, sensitive for bleeding polyps/CRC. FIT-DNA test (Cologuard): more sensitive than FIT alone. Flexible sigmoidoscopy every 5 years. BNYS role: screen every patient over 45 with family history, IBD, previous polyps, or CRC risk factors. Create a simple reminder protocol — asking "are you up to date with your colonoscopy?" saves lives. Document screening status in every patient over 45.

cards.title: Gut Microbiome — Overview & Key Phyla

alerts.label: Core concept
alerts.text: The gut microbiome contains ~38 trillion microorganisms — equal to the number of human cells. 99% of microbial genes are bacterial. Diversity is the single strongest indicator of microbiome health.

pearls.label: 🟡 F:B Ratio
pearls.text: Firmicutes:Bacteroidetes ratio. Elevated in obesity and type 2 diabetes. Dietary fibre ↑ Bacteroidetes. High-fat/sugar diet ↑ Firmicutes. F:B ratio can shift within days of dietary change — the microbiome is remarkably plastic.

cards.title: Leaky Gut (Intestinal Permeability)

alerts.label: Definition
alerts.text: Increased intestinal permeability = breakdown of tight junction proteins (occludin, claudin, ZO-1) allowing antigens, LPS, and microbes into systemic circulation → chronic low-grade inflammation.

pearls.label: 🟡 Clinical Pearl
pearls.text: L-Glutamine 5g twice daily is the best-evidenced supplement for tight junction repair. Zinc carnosine and butyrate (sodium butyrate 600mg/day) are strong adjuncts. Avoid NSAIDs throughout the repair phase — a single dose of ibuprofen can unmeasurably increase permeability within 1 hour.

steps.title: Triggers
steps.text: — NSAIDs, alcohol, gluten (in sensitive individuals), stress (cortisol), dysbiosis, food antigens, zonulin dysregulation

steps.title: Consequences
steps.text: — Systemic LPS (endotoxaemia) → TLR4 activation → TNF-α, IL-6 → insulin resistance, neuroinflammation, autoimmunity

steps.title: Assessment
steps.text: — Lactulose:mannitol ratio (urine), serum zonulin, LPS-binding protein, faecal calprotectin (inflammation marker)

steps.title: Repair protocol (5R)
steps.text: — Remove triggers → Replace (enzymes, HCl) → Re-inoculate (probiotics) → Repair (glutamine, zinc, butyrate) → Rebalance (stress, sleep)

cards.title: Probiotics — Evidence-Based Guide

alerts.label: Key rule
alerts.text: Probiotics are strain-specific — not interchangeable. The evidence for "Lactobacillus" does NOT apply across all Lactobacillus species or strains. Always cite the specific strain when recommending.

rows: AAD ★, traveller's diarrhoea, eczema in infants
C. diff prevention ★, AAD, IBD adjunct

pearls.label: 🟡 Timing matters
pearls.text: Take probiotics with or just before a meal (fat/protein slows gastric emptying) — survival to colon increases 4-fold compared to fasting. Refrigerated strains must stay cold. If using with antibiotics: take 2 hours apart, continue for 4 weeks post-course. L. rhamnosus GG + S. boulardii together is the most evidence-based combo for AAD prevention.

cards.title: Gut-Brain Axis — Mechanisms

alerts.label: Definition
alerts.text: The gut-brain axis is a bidirectional communication network linking the enteric nervous system (ENS), vagus nerve, HPA axis, immune system, and gut microbiome to the central nervous system.

rows: Gut microbiome signals reach brainstem directly
Stress → leaky gut → more stress (vicious cycle)

pearls.label: 🟡 Psychobiotics
pearls.text: Psychobiotics = probiotics with demonstrated mental health benefits. Best evidence: L. rhamnosus JB-1 (anxiety, GABA signalling via vagus), L. helveticus R0052 + B. longum R0175 (stress, cortisol, Lallemand formulation studied in RCTs), Lactobacillus casei Shirota (mood, sleep). Mechanism: vagus nerve, tryptophan/serotonin axis, GABA production.

cards.title: GERD — Naturopathic Assessment & Protocol

alerts.label: Alarm Features — Refer for Endoscopy Urgently
alerts.text: Dysphagia (difficulty swallowing), odynophagia (painful swallowing), unexplained weight loss, age over 55 with new onset dyspepsia, haematemesis or melaena. These require endoscopy before any naturopathic treatment.

pearls.label: 🟡 PPI Deprescribing
pearls.text: 70% of long-term PPI prescriptions have no ongoing indication. Step down: OD to every other day x 4 weeks, then H2 blocker x 4 weeks, then PRN antacid. Rebound acid hypersecretion occurs 2-4 weeks after stopping — warn patients this is temporary, not their original disease returning. DGL 380mg before meals throughout taper prevents symptom relapse significantly.

steps.title: Lifestyle First Line
steps.text: Elevate head of bed 15-20cm — most effective for nocturnal symptoms. Left lateral sleeping position. No eating within 3 hours of bed. Small frequent meals. Weight loss (each BMI unit reduction lowers GERD risk 10%). Stop smoking — dramatically reduces lower oesophageal sphincter pressure.

steps.title: Dietary Triggers
steps.text: Eliminate: chocolate, coffee and tea, alcohol, citrus fruits, tomatoes, fatty and fried foods. Peppermint relaxes LOS — avoid in GERD. Spicy food variable — not proven to worsen in RCTs but subjectively triggers in many patients.

steps.title: Naturopathic Treatments
steps.text: DGL liquorice 380mg before meals (stimulates mucus production, protects oesophageal mucosa). Aloe vera juice 50ml before meals (anti-inflammatory — small RCT positive). Slippery elm 400mg with water before meals (demulcent, coats oesophagus). Alkaline water pH 8.8 (denatures pepsin — pepsin is the main oesophageal irritant).

cards.title: Coeliac Disease — Diagnosis & Nutritional Management

alerts.label: Do NOT Start GFD Before Testing
alerts.text: A gluten-free diet normalises the diagnostic antibodies and biopsy findings — making diagnosis impossible. Always complete testing first. If patient has already started GFD, a gluten challenge (6g/day for 6 weeks) is required before testing.

rows: First-line test. ALWAYS check total IgA alongside — IgA deficiency gives false negative (occurs in 2% of coeliac).
If IgA deficient: use TTG-IgG or deamidated gliadin peptide IgG instead
Must still be eating gluten before biopsy. Marsh grading 0-3. Minimum 4 biopsies (patchy distribution).
Present in 99% of coeliac — if ABSENT, coeliac very unlikely. Used to rule OUT in equivocal cases.

steps.title: Gluten-Free Diet — What to Avoid
steps.text: ALL wheat (bread, pasta, cakes, chapati, roti, naan, soya sauce, beer), barley (barley water, malt extract), rye. SAFE: rice, corn, potato, quinoa, millet, sorghum, certified GF oats. Read ALL labels — gluten hides in soups, sauces, processed meats, medications, shared fryer chips.

steps.title: Nutritional Replenishment
steps.text: Commonly deficient at diagnosis: iron, folate, B12, Vit D3, calcium, zinc, magnesium. Check all at diagnosis. Iron bisglycinate 25mg with Vit C. Vit D3 4000 IU. Calcium 1200mg. B12 sublingual if low. Annual monitoring bloods thereafter.

Lactulose or glucose substrate. H2 rise >20ppm above baseline within 90 min = positive. 3-hour test required to detect methane. Prepare: no antibiotics for 4 weeks, no probiotics for 1 week, low-FODMAP diet for 24h.

Allicin (garlic extract) 450mg TDS — comparable to rifaximin (RCT). Oregano oil, berberine, neem. Elemental diet × 14 days (80–85% eradication — best evidence). Biofilm disruptors: NAC, serrapeptase.

pearls.label: 🟡 The MMC — why SIBO recurs without prokinetics

cards.title: Candida & Fungal Overgrowth — Assessment & Protocol

alerts.label: Candida Overgrowth — Overdiagnosed but Real
alerts.text: True Candida overgrowth (dysbiosis) exists — primarily in immunocompromised, post-antibiotic, or high-sugar-diet patients. Many "Candida" diagnoses are not supported by evidence. The clinical approach: diagnose carefully (stool culture, organic acids), treat with evidence-based antifungals, and address root causes.

rows: Recurrent vaginal thrush, oral thrush, nail fungal
Bloating, gas, fatigue, brain fog, sugar cravings
Comprehensive stool analysis with fungal culture

steps.title: Antifungal Protocol
steps.text: Caprylic acid 1–2g TID ★★ (coconut-derived C8 — disrupts fungal cell membrane). Undecylenic acid 500mg TID ★★. Oregano oil (carvacrol 75%) 200mg TID ★★★ — potent antifungal, anti-biofilm. Grapefruit seed extract 250mg TID ★★. Berberine 500mg TID ★★ (anti-Candida + glucose regulation — removes substrate). Saccharomyces boulardii 5×10⁹ CFU ★★★ — competitive exclusion. Rotation every 2–3 weeks prevents resistance.

steps.title: Anti-Candida Diet
steps.text: Remove: all sugars (including fruit juice, honey, alcohol), refined carbohydrates, yeast-containing foods (bread, beer, fermented foods — for first 4 weeks). Add: antifungal foods: garlic ★★★ (allicin), coconut oil (caprylic acid), raw apple cider vinegar (malic acid), cruciferous vegetables, olive oil. Duration: strict phase 4 weeks, then gradual reintroduction. Die-off reaction (Herxheimer): fatigue, headache worsening in first week — is expected, manage with hydration, activated charcoal 30 min away from supplements.

cards.title: Inflammatory Bowel Disease — Naturopathic Adjunct

alerts.label: IBD — Naturopathic as Adjunct to Conventional Only
alerts.text: Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis are serious immune-mediated conditions requiring gastroenterologist co-management. Biologics and immunomodulators prevent complications and surgery. Naturopathic role: reduce flare frequency, improve nutritional status, manage symptoms, support quality of life, and address lifestyle factors.

rows: ★★★ First-line induction for paediatric Crohn's — equivalent to corticosteroids
Elemental or polymeric formula as sole nutrition 6–8 weeks. BNYS: support adherence, introduce foods carefully after.
★★ IBD-AID trial: significant improvement in Crohn's and UC symptom scores
Eliminates: grains, refined sugars, most dairy. Includes: well-cooked vegetables, homemade yogurt, specific fruits. Strict 3–12 months.
★★★ UC maintenance: VSL#3 equivalent to mesalazine for mild-moderate UC remission maintenance
VSL#3 1 sachet BD. Contains 450 billion bacteria from 8 strains. Refrigerated. Continue through remission.
★★★ UC: curcumin 2g/day added to mesalazine significantly improves remission rates vs mesalazine alone
Curcumin 1g BD with piperine. BCM-95 formulation preferred (enhanced bioavailability). With meals.
★★ Crohn's maintenance: omega-3 reduces relapse rate in some RCTs
EPA+DHA 3–4g/day. Enteric-coated capsules reduce fishy burps and GI side effects.

pearls.label: 🟡 Nutritional Deficiency Screening in IBD
pearls.text: IBD causes malabsorption of almost everything. Screen and supplement: Iron (check ferritin — IV iron preferred over oral in active IBD — oral worsens mucosal inflammation), B12 methylcobalamin (ileal Crohn's — intrinsic factor absorption site), Vit D (deficiency worsens IBD prognosis and flare frequency — target 100–150 nmol/L), Zinc (depleted in diarrhoea — 25mg/day), Folate (methotrexate-induced depletion), Magnesium. Monitor: FBC, B12, folate, iron studies, Vit D, zinc, albumin every 6 months.

cards.title: Liver Disease — NAFLD to Cirrhosis

alerts.label: NAFLD — The Silent Epidemic
alerts.text: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) affects 25% of the global population and 30–40% of Indian adults. The spectrum: simple steatosis → NASH (with inflammation + fibrosis) → cirrhosis → hepatocellular carcinoma. NAFLD is strongly linked to insulin resistance — treat the root cause, not just the liver. BNYS is excellently positioned for NAFLD management.

rows: Often asymptomatic. ALT mildly ↑. Bright liver on ultrasound.
Weight loss 7–10% ★★★ (resolves steatosis). Low-fructose diet. Exercise. Berberine.
Fatigue, RUQ discomfort. ALT/AST ↑↑. FIB-4 score elevated.
Above + silymarin ★★★, Vit E 800IU/day ★★★ (PIVENS trial), omega-3, TUDCA, curcumin.
Hepatologist co-management. Naturopathic supportive: protein 1.2g/kg, zinc, magnesium, BCAA, low-sodium diet. Avoid hepatotoxic herbs.

pearls.label: 🟡 Coffee — Nature's Hepatoprotective Drink
pearls.text: 3–4 cups of coffee per day reduces: NAFLD risk 40%, cirrhosis risk 40%, liver cancer risk 40%, liver fibrosis progression 50%. The protective effects are from: chlorogenic acids (antioxidant), cafestol (NRF2 activation), caffeine (reversal of hepatic fibrosis in animal models). Both caffeinated and decaffeinated coffee are protective — but caffeinated shows stronger effects. Green tea catechins: also hepatoprotective (EGCG 400mg/day reduces liver enzymes in NAFLD ★★). This is the most evidence-based dietary recommendation in hepatology.

steps.title: NAFLD Naturopathic Protocol
steps.text: Silymarin (milk thistle) standardised to 70–80% silymarin 140mg TID ★★★ (reduces ALT/AST, improves histology in multiple RCTs). Berberine 500mg TID ★★★ (reduces hepatic fat via AMPK activation — comparable to pioglitazone in NASH RCT). Vit E (natural d-alpha tocopherol) 800 IU/day ★★★ (PIVENS trial: significant NASH histological improvement). TUDCA (tauroursodeoxycholic acid) 500mg BD ★★ (bile acid that reduces ER stress in hepatocytes). Omega-3 EPA+DHA 3–4g/day (reduces hepatic triglyceride by 15–20%). Curcumin 1000mg with piperine BD (anti-inflammatory, antifibrotic). Coffee 3+ cups/day ★★★ (most replicated hepatoprotective food — reduces fibrosis progression 40–50%).

steps.title: FIB-4 Score — Non-Invasive Fibrosis Assessment
steps.text: FIB-4 = (Age × AST) ÷ (Platelets × √ALT). <1.3: low fibrosis risk (F0–F1) — continue naturopathic management. 1.3–2.67: intermediate — FibroScan (transient elastography) recommended. >2.67: high risk of advanced fibrosis — hepatologist referral. Use FIB-4 at baseline and every 12 months to track treatment response. Free calculator available online. Avoids biopsy in most patients.

cards.title: Functional Dyspepsia & Gastroparesis

alerts.label: Functional Dyspepsia — Rome IV Criteria
alerts.text: Functional dyspepsia (FD) affects 20% of adults globally. Rome IV criteria: Bothersome postprandial fullness, early satiation, epigastric pain/burning — for 3+ months, no structural cause on endoscopy. Two subtypes: (1) Postprandial Distress Syndrome (PDS) — meal-related symptoms. (2) Epigastric Pain Syndrome (EPS) — pain/burning not meal-related. BNYS evidence base is strong for FD.

rows: ★★★ Multiple RCTs — comparable to cisapride for FD/IBS
20 drops TID before meals in water. Contains: bitter candy tuft, liquorice, chamomile, caraway, lemon balm, peppermint, angelica, celandine, milk thistle
Dual action: prokinetic (speeds gastric emptying) + spasmolytic (relaxes smooth muscle). Multi-herb synergy superior to individual herbs.

Ginger capsule 1000–1500mg with meals. Fresh ginger tea before meals. Ginger essential oil massage abdomen.
5-HT3 antagonism (antiemetic), D2 agonism (prokinetic), antiemetic via vagal modulation
★★★ IBS + FD. Enteric coating essential (avoids oesophageal relaxation and heartburn).

Calcium channel antagonist → smooth muscle relaxation → reduces visceral hypersensitivity

Compensates for reduced endogenous enzyme output. Improves nutrient extraction and reduces fermentation dyspepsia.

pearls.label: 🟡 Gastroparesis — Delayed Gastric Emptying
pearls.text: Gastroparesis: delayed gastric emptying without mechanical obstruction. Symptoms: postprandial fullness, nausea, vomiting undigested food, bloating, early satiety. Causes: diabetes (autonomic neuropathy) ★, post-viral, post-surgical, idiopathic. Diagnosis: gastric emptying scintigraphy (gold standard) or 13C-spirulina breath test. Naturopathic: Ginger ★★★ (improves antral contractions), Iberogast ★★, small frequent meals (6/day), low-fat/low-fibre diet (reduces gastric emptying delay), liquid/pureed meals, no carbonated drinks, upright posture after meals. Refer gastroenterology if: significant weight loss, recurrent vomiting, dehydration.

cards.title: Pancreatitis — Acute & Chronic

alerts.label: Acute Pancreatitis — Medical Emergency
alerts.text: Acute pancreatitis requires immediate hospital management. BNYS practitioners must recognise it and refer urgently. Never manage acute pancreatitis naturopathically — even mild cases require IV fluids, nil by mouth, and monitoring for complications (necrosis, ARDS, multi-organ failure).

rows: Mild: no organ failure, no local complications. Moderately severe: transient organ failure (<48hrs) or local complications. Severe: persistent organ failure (>48hrs)
PaO₂ <60mmHg, Age >55, Neutrophils >15,000, Calcium <2mmol/L, renal: urea >16mmol/L, Enzymes (LDH >600, AST >200), Albumin <32g/L, Sugar (glucose >10mmol/L)
PANCREAS mnemonic. ≥3 within 48hrs = severe. Predicts ICU need.
I: Idiopathic. G: Gallstones ★ (40%). E: Ethanol ★ (30%). T: Trauma, Triglycerides (>11mmol/L), drugs, hypercalcaemia, ERCP, viral
Gallstones and alcohol = 70% of cases. TG >11 = severe hypertriglyceridaemic pancreatitis (treat TG with fibrates + omega-3)

steps.title: Chronic Pancreatitis — Naturopathic Support
steps.text: Enzyme replacement: pancreatic enzymes (lipase 25,000–50,000 IU) with every meal and snack — essential for fat digestion and malabsorption. Fat-soluble vitamin supplementation: A, D, E, K2 — all malabsorbed in exocrine insufficiency. Medium-chain triglycerides (MCT oil): absorbed without pancreatic enzymes — use instead of LCT fats. Antioxidant protocol: Vit C 1g, Vit E 400IU, selenium 200mcg, methionine 2g, beta-carotene 9000IU ★★ — reduces pain episodes (Banerjee antioxidant cocktail). Complete alcohol cessation ★★★ — most important modifiable factor for progression. Smoking cessation ★★★ (doubles progression rate to carcinoma). Pain management: curcumin, acupuncture.

cards.title: Colorectal Cancer Prevention

alerts.label: CRC — 90% Preventable With Lifestyle + Screening
alerts.text: Colorectal cancer is the 3rd most common cancer globally. 90% arise from adenomatous polyps over 10–15 years — a long preventable window. BNYS practitioners can powerfully reduce patient CRC risk through dietary and lifestyle prescription, and ensure appropriate screening referrals.

rows: 150 min moderate exercise/week ★★★ (reduces CRC risk 24%). Exercise also improves survival post-diagnosis.
Reduce to <1 drink/day or abstain. Folate partially mitigates alcohol-related CRC risk.
Limit processed meat (biggest risk — hot dogs, sausage, bacon). Replace red meat with fish, poultry, legumes.
Dietary fibre 30–35g/day ★★★. Every 10g extra fibre = 10% ↓ CRC risk. Whole grains, legumes, vegetables.
Calcium 1000mg/day from food + supplement ★★ (reduces polyp recurrence). Vit D 1000–4000 IU (improves calcium effect).

steps.title: The Gut Microbiome & CRC Connection
steps.text: Fusobacterium nucleatum found in 90% of CRC tumours — promotes tumour growth via FadA adhesin + Wnt signalling. High fibre diet selects against Fusobacterium and enriches butyrate-producing bacteria (Faecalibacterium prausnitzii, Roseburia). Butyrate is the primary colonocyte fuel AND a histone deacetylase inhibitor — epigenetically suppresses tumour genes. Resistant starch (cooked-cooled rice and potatoes, green bananas) is the most potent butyrate substrate — fermented selectively by protective bacteria. Target: diverse, high-plant, high-fibre microbiome.

steps.title: CRC Screening — Ensure Patients Are Screened
steps.text: Indian recommendations (ICMR): colonoscopy every 10 years from age 45 (40 if family history). Faecal immunochemical test (FIT): annually — non-invasive, sensitive for bleeding polyps/CRC. FIT-DNA test (Cologuard): more sensitive than FIT alone. Flexible sigmoidoscopy every 5 years. BNYS role: screen every patient over 45 with family history, IBD, previous polyps, or CRC risk factors. Create a simple reminder protocol — asking "are you up to date with your colonoscopy?" saves lives. Document screening status in every patient over 45.

cards._legacyHtml:
CRC — 90% Preventable With Lifestyle + Screening
Colorectal cancer is the 3rd most common cancer globally. 90% arise from adenomatous polyps over 10–15 years — a long preventable window. BNYS practitioners can powerfully reduce patient CRC risk through dietary and lifestyle prescription, and ensure appropriate screening referrals.
